seoTitle: How to Change Fonts in Google Docs (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Change fonts in Google Docs using the toolbar dropdown or the extended web font library, and save your favorite typeface as the default style.
intro: Changing your document's typeface in Google Docs is straightforward once you know where the extended font library is hiding. This guide covers selecting text and switching fonts through the toolbar dropdown, browsing hundreds of additional styles, and setting your preferred choice as the default so future documents start off looking the way you want.

steps.heading: Select the text you want to change
steps.body: Highlight a word, paragraph, or your entire document using standard cursor selection before opening the font menu.

steps.heading: Open the Font dropdown
steps.body: Click the Font selector on the toolbar to browse your currently available typefaces, including common defaults like Arial and Times New Roman.

steps.heading: Access the extended font library
steps.body: Choose More fonts at the top of the dropdown to unlock and filter hundreds of additional Google Fonts.

steps.heading: Filter and preview styles
steps.body: Browse by category, such as Serif, Sans-Serif, or Handwriting, to narrow down options that fit your document's tone.

steps.heading: Apply your chosen font
steps.body: Select the new typeface to apply it to your highlighted text, then review size scaling and character spacing.

steps.heading: Save as your default style
steps.body: Use the Format menu to save your new font configuration as the default so it automatically applies to future documents.

faq.question: Why can't I find a specific font in the standard dropdown?
faq.answer: The default dropdown only shows a limited set; open More fonts to search the full library of hundreds of additional typefaces.

faq.question: Will changing fonts affect existing formatting like bold or italics?
faq.answer: No, changing the font family keeps other formatting like bold, italics, and color intact on your selected text.

faq.question: Can I set a different default font for each new document?
faq.answer: Default styles are saved per document through paragraph style settings, so each file can have its own default font configuration.

faq.question: Do custom fonts show correctly for people who open my shared document?
faq.answer: Yes, since Google Docs renders fonts from its own web library, collaborators see the same typeface regardless of what's installed on their device.
